title: Manager Mode · Scrapbook
notes:
Human-readable catalog of the lore_tokens that Manager Mode surfaces across the summer of '98.  A future scrapbook UI can enumerate this file, cross-reference each entry against GauntletState.state.lore_tokens_revealed, and render the discovered ones with a colored dot next to their title.  Tokens not yet earned render as '· · ·' silhouettes.

Three tiers:
  · guests (5)     — served each rare guest at least once
  · modifiers (6)  — survived each unusual shift condition
  · nights (3)     — met a per-night threshold (any night)
  · summers (2)    — accumulated across a whole run
  · endings (3)    — reached one of the Manager-only endings

entries.title: The regional manager's walk-through
entries.blurb: The suit walked in on a Wednesday, bought bottled water, said thank you, walked out.  Sam kept the receipt for a year.

entries.title: Three high-schoolers, three drinks
entries.blurb: They were going to a party.  Sam did not, at 24, feel old.  Sam felt, in a way Sam could not identify, sober.

entries.title: Directions to Cannon Beach
entries.blurb: The wife asked, the husband bought the map, both left grateful.  Sam thought, later, about how much of the summer was directions.

entries.title: A tall and a tip that meant it
entries.blurb: 'You keep a good store, kid.'  Sam wrote the biker's actual sentence down in the ledger, in pencil, in the margin.

entries.title: The church lady's ginger ale
entries.blurb: She offered a prayer.  Sam accepted it.  Neither Sam nor the church lady thought this odd.

entries.title: A full moon on the water
entries.blurb: Tips were doubled.  Nobody remembers why, but every clerk on the coast will tell you this if you ask.

entries.title: The night deputy's look
entries.blurb: He walked through, nodded, walked out.  Sam did not know what was being looked for.  Sam passed anyway.

entries.title: The counterfeit twenty
entries.blurb: Somebody left a bad twenty in the register last shift.  Sam ate the loss and finished the night in the black.

entries.title: County fair traffic, zero walkouts
entries.blurb: The Tillamook fair opened; 101 was busy; Sam still served every walk-in cleanly.  A minor miracle.

entries.title: Eight-and-eight-and-eight, zero walkouts
entries.blurb: Snyder's truck skipped.  The cooler ran short.  Sam still served every walk-in cleanly.  A less minor miracle.

entries.title: Thirty dollars in the rain
entries.blurb: It rained all night.  The parking lot was mirroring the fluorescents.  Sam still rang thirty.

entries.title: A clean night
entries.blurb: One shift, zero walkouts.  It happens.  It doesn't always.

entries.title: A hundred-dollar night
entries.blurb: One shift, $100 rung.  Not the biggest ever · a good one.

entries.title: A five-dollar tip night
entries.blurb: One shift, tip jar past five bucks.  Sam bought a coffee on the way home with it.

entries.title: Three of the five
entries.blurb: The summer had, when you counted it, three of the five rare visitors.  A wide summer.

entries.title: All five
entries.blurb: The regional manager.  Three high-schoolers.  A lost couple.  A biker.  A church lady.  A summer where nobody unusual was missed.

entries.title: The summer, wide
entries.blurb: Five different unusual conditions survived cleanly.  The summer of '98, in retrospect, was not the average summer.

entries.title: Owns it now
entries.blurb: The $4,200 threshold cleared, the offer made, Jules said yes on Tuesday.  Five years later the store is still the store.

entries.title: Ran the clean ledger
entries.blurb: Twelve nights, zero walkouts.  The costs, quiet.  The wren, heard later.

entries.title: Closed at 3:47
entries.blurb: Three bad nights in a row broke something.  Sam ended the shift, wrote a note, walked to the estuary, came back the next night.  Held.
